# UC-17.1.23 · SonicWall NGFW IPS and Gateway Anti-Virus Alerts by Signature

## Description

SonicWall deep packet inspection generates high-fidelity IPS and gateway anti-malware events. Clustering by signature and endpoint pair reveals active exploitation attempts, worm propagation, or policy gaps where expected traffic is misclassified.

## Value

Prioritises SOC review toward the noisiest defensive actions at the perimeter without waiting for endpoint telemetry lag.

## Implementation

Forward SonicWall syslog to Splunk via SC4S or a forwarder; set `linebreaking` per vendor guidance. Install Splunkbase app 6203 for field extractions. Validate whether your deployment uses `dell:sonicwall` or `dell:sonicwall:firewall`. Create lookups for pen-test windows and vulnerability-scanner subnets.

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw (sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" OR sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall") earliest=-24h
| eval sig=coalesce(signature, msg, cat, threat_name)
| eval src_ip=coalesce(src_ip, src, c_src)
| eval dest_ip=coalesce(dest_ip, dst, c_dst)
| where match(lower(sig),"(?i)intrusion|ips|gateway anti|virus|sid") OR match(_raw,"(?i)priority.*(high|critical)")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, sig
| where count>=5
| sort -count
| head 40
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (signature, sources, destinations), Heatmap (time × signature), MITRE tagging overlay in ES if available.

## References

- [Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-connect-for-syslog/main/sources/vendor/Dell/sonicwall/)
- [Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6203)
